title: Odyssey, Book 12: Scylla and Charybdis
description: Book 12 of Homer's Odyssey: Scylla and Charybdis.
introduction: Book 12 of the Odyssey: Scylla and Charybdis. The Greek text follows the standard Homeric vulgate.

translation.text: They returned to Circe, who warned them of the Sirens, of Scylla and Charybdis, and of the cattle of the Sun. Odysseus plugged his men's ears with wax and had himself tied to the mast to hear the Sirens' song. They passed between six-headed Scylla and the whirlpool Charybdis. On Thrinacia, his starving men killed the Sun's cattle. Zeus destroyed the ship. Only Odysseus survived, clinging to the mast.
